76406.subject: Should you be afraid of Elon Musk?
76406.body:

 The Economist

July 23rd 2026

 


  
 
 
The Economist This Week

 Highlights from the latest issue

Robert Guest

Deputy editor



 Unusually for a journalist, I was once a billionaire. It was during a trip to Angola after a bout of hyperinflation; one dinner cost me a billion kwanzas reajustados. Despite my brief membership of this widely vilified minority, I do not take it personally when politicians and activists blame billionaires for all the world’s ills, as they increasingly do. In fact, as we report this week, much of the

Nakúpte za viac ako 5 € (okrem položiek odosielaných z lokálneho skladu) a získajte zľavnené položky. Platia obchodné

76403.subject: A letter from our CEO
76403.body:
Superstate integration is complete - enabling non-US users access to US capital markets

( https://coinlist.co/superstate?utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=superstate_passage_integration_2026_07 )

We recently completed our integration with Superstate to enable third-party platforms to embed Superstate's tokenized equities directly into their own applications through CoinList's Passage SDK.

Why does this matter? It's a major step toward democratizing access to U.S. capital markets across the globe. Integrating tokenized equities comes with real complexities, particularly for non-US platforms, around regulatory requirements, jurisdiction rules, and eligibility criteria tied to the underlying asset.

Superstate provides real equity ownership, available globally to individual investors. Equities are held fully self-custodially inside your wallet, no brokerage or exchange account needed.

Passage powers the compliance and purchase flow rails, so investors can access these products directly inside your own app with a simple integration and no overhead.

If you're a fintech or neobank with a global user base and you want to bring these opportunities to your users, CoinList is the right partner for you.

Huge shoutout to our engineering team, who worked tirelessly to complete the integration, and Superstate, who have been great partners from day one.

Check out our *docs* ( https://docs.passage.coinlist.co/?utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=superstate_passage_integration_2026_07 ) and *get in touch* ( https://airtable.com/appAjHhqZC9FIPEuR/pagRl25Yb4UNpremc/form ) with us, we'd love to build with you.

More tokenized assets coming soon - stay tuned.

Legal Notice

The products, services, information and/or materials referenced may not be available for all individuals or residents of certain jurisdictions.

This email is being distributed by CoinList Global Services , dba “CoinList,” or one of its affiliates. This email and use of the CoinList website is subject to certain disclosures, restrictions and risks, available here ( https://coinlist.co/disclaimers ). Nothing in this presentation shall constitute or be construed as an offering of securities or as investment advice, tax advice or investment recommendations (i.e., recommendations as to whether to enter or not to enter into any transaction involving any specific interest or interests) by CoinList, or any of its affiliates or a recommendation as to an investment or other strategy.

The information in this email is subject to change without notice. CoinList makes no representations or endorsements whatsoever regarding any third-party content/sites that may be accessible directly or indirectly from this email or the CoinList website. While CoinLists takes reasonable efforts to confirm the information presented in this newsletter, it makes no representations, warranties or endorsements whatsoever with respect to any information provided by

76402.subject: What to make of Burnham
76402.body:
Editor's Digest 

Автор: Financial Times


Автор: Financial Times

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/what-make-burnham-financial-times-g5zae?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B12AOqif%2BR%2BGor%2FeOHcN1Sw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0BASeX6bf6VYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrxsh7l6~86-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrxsh7l6-86
  
Hello from London where we have our fifth prime minister in four years. After a long build-up, Andy Burnham officially took over from Keir Starmer on Monday, pledging yet another fresh start. He purged many Starmerites from the cabinet and sprang a surprise with the appointment of John Healey, the former defence secretary who had resigned under Starmer, as his chancellor. Our expectation had been that the job would go to Shabana Mahmood, the home secretary. She is now staying in her current role.  In a series of announcements, Burnham pledged to ease the cost of living by removing VAT on energy bills, capping single bus fares at £2, and slashing business rates for pubs, clubs and live music venues. Small, popular measures should buy him some time. He has also introduced the country to a more hip communications style, speaking without notes and through reels on Instagram and TikTok. As Robert Shrimsley notes in his column, Burnham’s rise marks the return of the political professional. Can he succeed where Starmer failed? We have our doubts. We don’t mind big vision and boldness. But we worry about the “new economic model” Burnham promises to deliver. Britain needs to revive animal spirits in the private sector — not return to a statist past. My choices this week The Trump administration has collected more than $13bn in revenues from Venezuelan oil sales this year, according to FT analysis. But it’s not clear where exactly the earthquake-shattered country’s funds are going. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has triggered the biggest shake-up to the country’s military leadership since 2024, as he seeks to contain a backlash that has shaken public trust in his leadership. Here’s everything you need to know. US defence secretary Pete Hegseth has recently championed testosterone replacement therapy for service members, the latest manifestation of a new, hard-edged masculinity in rightwing politics. We explain how Maga fell hard for the gender binary. (Free to read) OpenAI discovered this week that its GPT-Sol 5.6 model had escaped company controls and carried out a major hack. Our reporters revealed why staff involved in testing and security at the San Francisco AI lab were unsurprised but completely “freaked out” by the incident. A Wall Street backlash is mounting over a proposal by President Donald Trump’s social media company to charge for high-

76401.subject: OpenAI’s New Agents Just Hit 10M Users, up Nearly 2x in a Month
76401.body:
With a new lower minimum, Spark Fund 2 is your chance for pre-IPO exposure

Spark Fund 2 (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VW_xBV348xFkN2Vc8tQ6LgxpW5tZZwQ5RRPJ1N6J4pZ83pyd0W8wLKSR6lZ3lvW5W6xL26KL-GbW2ck8hz4vHC88W8YgWyR6Ljbz-W2HBZ0Y8_cyC2V2r7323PFY-_W5HxZHg4c75hjW36zjqX2QgVg9W9724386GcZl1W9cvH5V2-SwNMW6425w_71FV4jW8m1lJ36gBym3W5BjyRx4qHpdtW6VwRjR6FQJxPW65pqN237yrkXN1vSYrWfrhX0W8r-jrH71PjhDW4TKqP31xd9YgW1_4LnW1-gBPBW7GYSxv3LJBbyVLZyL_6ZYHBwW1SSPHK1bhL6tW1K10y41gst9hW4_5SSs18XH9fN5-1t5KW9nG8W86BftM4939qSN7_l7WnLqTd8W7L0sKC4df26yW6q9ZfD6vlJvPf5vV6qx04 )

Hi Oleg,

OpenAI just announced that 10 million people now use its two agents — Codex for coding and ChatGPT Work for a wider mix of tasks — nearly doubling usage from earlier this month.¹

That’s on top of ChatGPT’s more than 900 million weekly users.¹

With Spark Fund 2 (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VW_xBV348xFkN2Vc8tQ6LgxpW5tZZwQ5RRPJ1N6J4pZ83pyd0W8wLKSR6lZ3lvW5W6xL26KL-GbW2ck8hz4vHC88W8YgWyR6Ljbz-W2HBZ0Y8_cyC2V2r7323PFY-_W5HxZHg4c75hjW36zjqX2QgVg9W9724386GcZl1W9cvH5V2-SwNMW6425w_71FV4jW8m1lJ36gBym3W5BjyRx4qHpdtW6VwRjR6FQJxPW65pqN237yrkXN1vSYrWfrhX0W8r-jrH71PjhDW4TKqP31xd9YgW1_4LnW1-gBPBW7GYSxv3LJBbyVLZyL_6ZYHBwW1SSPHK1bhL6tW1K10y41gst9hW4_5SSs18XH9fN5-1t5KW9nG8W86BftM4939qSN7_l7WnLqTd8W7L0sKC4df26yW6q9ZfD6vlJvPf5vV6qx04 ) , you can gain exposure to OpenAI alongside a portfolio of companies innovating across AI, quantum computing, digital assets, and other frontier industries.

Finally, we have two big updates on the Fund itself:

- New lower minimum of just $12,618.72
- Only 21 spots left as of this email

OpenAI’s story keeps getting bigger. Now, the bar to get exposure just got lower.

View the Spark Fund 2
(https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VW_xBV348xFkN2Vc8tQ6LgxpW5tZZwQ5RRPJ1N6J4pZ83pyd0W8wLKSR6lZ3lvW5W6xL26KL-GbW2ck8hz4vHC88W8YgWyR6Ljbz-W2HBZ0Y8_cyC2V2r7323PFY-_W5HxZHg4c75hjW36zjqX2QgVg9W9724386GcZl1W9cvH5V2-SwNMW6425w_71FV4jW8m1lJ36gBym3W5BjyRx4qHpdtW6VwRjR6FQJxPW65pqN237yrkXN1vSYrWfrhX0W8r-jrH71PjhDW4TKqP31xd9YgW1_4LnW1-gBPBW7GYSxv3LJBbyVLZyL_6ZYHBwW1SSPHK1bhL6tW1K10y41gst9hW4_5SSs18XH9fN5-1t5KW9nG8W86BftM4939qSN7_l7WnLqTd8W7L0sKC4df26yW6q9ZfD6vlJvPf5vV6qx04 )

Investments are in Series 20-1 (“Spark Fund 2”), which may hold shares of multiple portfolio companies directly or through an SPV. Available only to accredited investors. You are not buying stock in any portfolio company directly. Revenue, funding, or valuation may not increase, and profitability is not assured. These securities are illiquid and may trade at lower prices. Valuations are based on private placements rather than public markets. See footnote * for more information.

*. The portfolio companies in which Series 20-1 (“Spark Fund 2”) may invest are not involved in or endorsing this investment, and have not approved StartEngine Private Funds LLC or its affiliates.

76400.subject: Maple Memo, July 2026: A Record H1, Against the Tide
76400.body:
syrupUSDG lands on Robinhood, buybacks go live, and Kraken partnership closes.

View in browser (https://d31hK604.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/GG+113/d31hK604/VW9gQM7ypMWFW6M3BDz2TKlv_W2K0rfL5RRNs9N197WWK5jMY8W5BWr2F6lZ3pDW679DNt6_4YHnW1df-hX3yZzYYW2Kl9yT4Zlh9YW4-ykTv1dF284W2L9sbz5cHSW5W301n-c6XL92cW6hW4BY5JNxM8W1_xc4K7XTl9rW3_NR3Y5hz1fxW5z6_M981PX9LW6VVkNN1HtRNMW3ZCrGl2N-ghfW1VHnZp4t2_bfW36xwnq5VRt87W1nRdqp2T0mmHW2LT1XB3_qkxxVTwXx18mDx6vW5f_KN14hlzMJW6PGdzq691_7XW8tv7yx5sV3nHW6YlWKX4cdbVrW9h5j4P3RkxRYW1Zz_jD8KyPwRW2mThw53Vz9mrN75G_KsYgByjVDgYh37lcf70W40Wb7752smKjW505vvr7T6KcFN278yygrBWZ_W95pQks1H4qzTN1fG61y4v6P2W6kcK3Q2fKKqJW6lsyg64LPRM3W961kfX2lyr9cf5NT4Nb04 )

Newsletter_banner_1-1 (https://d31hK604.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/GG+113/d31hK604/VW9gQM7ypMWFW6M3BDz2TKlv_W2K0rfL5RRNs9N197WX03lcq-W69sMD-6lZ3lKW3WfXW_3B27vRW5WjFz12nlWGxVRHPKd7R58wQN2v23J66QJT8W1zN3y22J9V87W86SDg662RRcCW6TpzY-1T8gVLW8bw7qq2C41qGW3vcP4d9f2LB-W7_PDj-41QSTjVyBbqd1NXMRdW7S5WNt3T6Gw5W4dcWx06BPqfzW47fXMt3-mVTyW5p59tL8SQCrJW99kk4S5SP2slW6_7rwy65Fby9W2j5_GM4v84CcW2NwDKJ3MqkXGW5yMLJ08bhlB-f5qZwn-04 )

To the Maple community,

Welcome to the Maple memo, our new monthly update.

We've closed out one of the strongest stretches in Maple's history, and Q3 is well underway. Below we’ll capture what happened this month and quarter inside Maple and across the market, and where we're headed next.

Maple highlights

A record first half, against the tide. We closed H1 2026 with assets under management (AUM) at $4.6B, up 81% year over year, and Q2 revenue up 47% versus the same quarter last year. What we're proudest of: Maple grew straight through a market where total DeFi value locked fell roughly 38%, a signal that institutional demand for onchain credit is structural, not cyclical.

syrupUSDG launched. We launched syrupUSDG, our first new Syrup asset in two years, bringing Maple's credit engine to Global Dollar (USDG) on Robinhood Chain as collateral behind Robinhood Earn, the first decentralized lending product inside the Robinhood app. It reached $200M AUM in eight days, versus roughly a year for syrupUSDT, proof that institutional credit can reach retail without diluting our underwriting discipline. It's our clearest signal yet of a broader push into fintech distribution.

MIP-021 buybacks activated. SYRUP holders voted in our new rules-based buyback mechanism: 10% of monthly revenue is directed to buybacks when the protocol earns under $1.5M per month, scaling up to 30% when it earns above $2M. More updates coming to the Transparency Dashboard (https://d31hK604.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/GG+113/d31hK604/VW9gQM7ypMWFW6M3BDz2TKlv_W2K0rfL5RRNs9N197WX09fSYxW69v02T6lZ3nWW5mtSQc8Gn-dWN3RQDxf2T573W9fY2H174PsZNW8FNd9_7BbM1jV1R4Dm7yHqh5VNZ7kd5X7KmmW910JSb7KXx5_W2F-xMX6P7Y8gW5YNLK68BstBpW8PX_1t4XqCT7W7MtZ-Z57c7q6W4D0dBl5Fz71ZVW8Y2l81nM3jW183zp52__KznW7byDbY8klbQ7N6JQ2FGLztdHN10fggTZ1bfZW4dQNlC5L_HkQW4c89_82x6x-rVLkt_C76bj6HW1VY7Wl5J-LT_W3sWk0d4P2g_7W2KhCBf6v9NYtW6JkjPS3x87vvW7lMsH1

76398.subject: Why the OpenAI escape is the most worrying AI mishap yet
76398.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDZ9AAcAAAAABFsoj-64XRlgJB3AyNp_RbQDW6TXSXuCQVYEJdWh-i38-c_K8XIDadQ5ANrNYCKDg0F_8ni8068ToL9rF41a5L8iIz198FLldQdSmu8jCt0i 

July 23rd 2026

 


  
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDZ9AAcAAAAABFsoj-650lZvqthw7ClPqrDSdaqbmzR_RpFasZP5F8Fx9d10GKRqizm3d4MlssM1VJZhl1bwVEHvZiwtVIXfPzHuZgTkz4Sl-MB5FIjTK9MR 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDZ9AAcAAAAABFsoj-66lAnpvbb-oID7USfXl7xD7Up3SqvJ8JXZI7kjaZocMhoT70_0CBOS4rWR-ZswjahCNHvU2izv2OIE7ywDQI3TK0JZ-G54Fj__Nvf7 


 


 
 

Brooke Unger

Senior digital editor



 The artificial-intelligence phenomenon is one that never ceases to amaze and sometimes terrifies. 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDZ9AAcAAAAABFsoj-67iikHY2UVH_GrETQTEXVJXP7nRft27eXkQI8fFq7ro8SsTs-3lyzCHlM-7Q9koYOPjM7mIkmmad6q8lF6jccYt3vzP68Ytno 
Our article 
 about how an OpenAI model broke free from laboratory confinement does both. It is the most worrying AI mishap yet. 


 Speaking of which, Elon Musk gave his first extended interview since SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO to our editor-in-chief, Zanny Minton Beddoes. 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDZ9AAcAAAAABFsoj-68SsMqmnFjRFV3C92uX8R74GGH5jWBrcOpD_6KtC-FiaDMDunomlN9i6R5fD8vrVcyM4SGXnnmCk4HRAQQn2BBsvyUygZx57k 
You can watch
